cases.question: What does CoefficientMatix._vecX2M build for the Gaussian model?
cases.gold_answer: A 4-equation × 10-unknowns matrix mapping pRF parameters to neighbourhood coefficients.
cases.model_answer:
CoefficientMatix._vecX2M builds the 4-row block of the design matrix M (one row each for the Gaussian value e and its partials de/dx, de/dy, de/dsigma) used by the modified linear regression / GLM step on the sampling grid. Each row is assembled from the 10-term polynomial expansion of a neighbor point vecX (x², y², sigma², 2xy, 2xz, 2yz, x, y, z, 1 and the corresponding derivative coefficients). 

The resulting stacked M (num_neighbors × 4 rows by 10 columns) is later passed to np.linalg.pinv for the per-grid-point coefficient solve.
